changes_summary:
Two changes addressing the flagged gaps:

1. **Description flow (flag: effect-metadata-description)**: Added `description` field to `ParameterDefinition` type. Updated LLM prompt (Step 4) to generate both a `// DESCRIPTION:` line (well-written effect description) and per-parameter `description` fields. Updated `extractEffectCodeAndMeta()` to parse the description. Updated EffectCreatorPanel (Step 6) to store `generatedDescription` in state, use it instead of `prompt.trim()` when saving metadata, and display it in the UI. Kept a fallback to `prompt.trim()` if the LLM doesn't produce a description.

2. **Default params at all apply sites (flag: clip-effect-state-defaults)**: Expanded Step 10 into two sub-sections (10a/10b) that enumerate all four call sites in ClipPanel that write effect objects: three dropdown `onValueChange` handlers (~lines 135, 172, 209) and the `onSaved` callback (~line 260). Each now looks up the resource's schema and includes `getDefaultValues()` in the written params. Added Step 11 covering the three equivalent call sites in BulkClipPanel (~lines 251, 280, 307). Updated `onSaved` callback signature to pass `defaultParams` from EffectCreatorPanel.
questions: Should the generated effect description be user-editable in the creator panel, or read-only (re-generated each time the LLM updates the code)?
success_criteria: TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit`) with all new types and extended interfaces
LLM-generated effects include a `parameterSchema` array (with per-parameter descriptions) and a well-written `description` string in the edge function response
ParameterControls renders the correct control type (slider/select/switch/color) for each parameter definition, showing label and description
Creator panel preview updates in real-time when tuneable parameter values change
The LLM-generated effect description is stored in metadata (not the user's prompt text)
Parameter schema is persisted in the effect resource's JSONB metadata
Per-clip parameter values are stored in `clip.entrance.params` / `clip.exit.params` / `clip.continuous.params`
Default param values are written to clips at all apply sites: ClipPanel dropdowns (3), ClipPanel onSaved (1), BulkClipPanel dropdowns (3)
ClipPanel shows parameter controls when a custom effect with schema is selected on a clip
Effect component receives `params` prop at render time and the rendered output reflects param values
Existing effects (built-in and custom without params) continue to work unchanged
Parameter values persist across page reload (stored in timeline config JSON)
assumptions: Immediate/optimistic updates for param changes in ClipPanel (matching existing clip property patterns like opacity, volume)
The `// PARAMS: [...]` and `// DESCRIPTION: ...` comment conventions for LLM output are safer than a JSON envelope for code-generation models
Empty `parameterSchema` (or absent field) means no tuneable params — fully backward compatible with existing effects
No database migration needed: `parameterSchema` goes in existing JSONB `metadata` column on `resources` table; `params` goes in existing clip JSON fields
Built-in effects will NOT get tuneable parameters in this iteration — controls only render for custom effects with a schema
When an effect's schema changes, existing clip param values are merged: valid values kept, new params get defaults, removed params are silently ignored (the component won't read them)
The Groq/Kimi K2 model can reliably generate the `// PARAMS:` and `// DESCRIPTION:` annotations — will add a fallback that defaults to empty schema/empty string if parsing fails
BulkClipPanel does not render ParameterControls — bulk operations set defaults; per-clip tuning happens in ClipPanel
